---
title: "Projects"
subtitle: "Research projects and data analysis portfolio"
toc: true
---

## Active Research Projects

### Caregiver Strain in Gastrointestinal Chronic Complex Conditions (GI CCCs)

::: {.project-card}
**Status:** Active | **Funding:** *To be added*

Families caring for children with GI CCCs face unique challenges including complex medical regimens, frequent hospitalizations, and significant lifestyle modifications. This project aims to:

- Develop and validate instruments to measure caregiver strain
- Identify modifiable factors associated with caregiver strain
- Design interventions to reduce caregiver strain

**Key Methods:** Survey development, psychometric validation, mixed methods
:::

---

### Clinical Outcomes in Pediatric Short Bowel Syndrome

::: {.project-card}
**Status:** Active | **Funding:** *NA*

Leveraging multi-center registry data to understand long-term outcomes in children with short bowel syndrome. Research questions include:

- Predictors of intestinal adaptation and enteral autonomy
- Risk factors for complications including catheter-related bloodstream infections
- Health-related quality of life outcomes

**Key Methods:** Cohort studies, survival analysis, 
:::

---

## Data Analysis Portfolio

### Interactive Visualizations

*Coming soon* - Interactive data visualizations and dashboards demonstrating analytical capabilities.

---

### Code & Reproducibility

Selected code repositories and reproducible research materials:

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal" icon=false}
**GitHub Repository**
Analysis code for published research projects.
[View on GitHub](https://github.com/agawulab){.btn .btn-sm .btn-outline-primary}
:::

---

## Tools & Methods

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### Statistical Analysis
- R / RStudio
- Stata

### Machine Learning
- tidymodels
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### Data Visualization
- ggplot2
- Shiny
- Quarto

### Other Tools
- REDCap
- Git/GitHub
- SQL
- LaTeX
:::

:::

---

## Collaborations

We welcome collaborations with researchers, clinicians, and patient advocates interested in improving outcomes for children with GI CCCs and their families.

Interested in collaborating? [Contact us](about.qmd#contact).
